content.body: Jake Archibald: did an editorial pass. Will let someone else verify the logic.
content.formatted_body: Jake Archibald: did an editorial pass. Will let someone else verify the logic.

content.body: annevk: are you happy with it landing without someone from WebKit verifying the logic?
content.formatted_body: @annevk:matrix.org: are you happy with it landing without someone from WebKit verifying the logic?

content.body: Yeah, that seems okay. If we hit issues when implementing we can always raise those at that point, but that is hopefully unlikely given the amount of vetting it will have had by then. (Assuming people still actually read the specification.)

foolip: seems like a long shot, but do you perhaps remember how the `muted` attribute of media elements is supposed to work? The description is essentially prose, which isn't great. It kinda suggests that script mutations should not work, but we don't really distinguish those from the parser setting attributes in the specification.
content.formatted_body: foolip: seems like a long shot, but do you perhaps remember how the muted attribute of media elements is supposed to work? The description is essentially prose, which isn't great. It kinda suggests that script mutations should not work, but we don't really distinguish those from the parser setting attributes in the specification.

content.body: The content attribute is reflected as `defaultMuted` and only affects the real `muted` IDL attribute at one point.
content.formatted_body: The content attribute is reflected as defaultMuted and only affects the real muted IDL attribute at one point.

content.body: (independent folks like Luke Warlow are looking at it)
content.formatted_body: (independent folks like @lwarlow:igalia.com are looking at it)

content.body: foolip: I think specifically the problem is this sentence: "When a media element is created, if the element has a muted content attribute specified, then the muted IDL attribute should be set to true"
content.formatted_body: foolip: I think specifically the problem is this sentence: "When a media element is created, if the element has a muted content attribute specified, then the muted IDL attribute should be set to true"
